about: ARID gold query set (KAN-24 starter). Each row = a natural-language question + the chunk(s) that correctly answer it (the 'relevant set'). Used to score embedding models via Recall@k / MRR (KAN-25). Run the same set over each model's retrieval and compare.
provenance: Drafted by Jarvis 2026-06-25 by PATTERN-MATCHING symbol names in chunks.jsonl, NOT by reading function bodies. Every row is status 'unverified-name-match' until an ARID specialist (Ari/Kieran) opens the target and confirms it. Verification job = KAN-24.
grading_rules: A query may have MORE THAN ONE correct chunk; list ALL of them in 'expected' or a model that retrieves the other valid chunk gets wrongly marked a miss.
Per row, set 'status' to one of: confirmed | fix-target (replace with the better chunk) | toss (bad question).
Target size ~30-50 questions spread across subsystems (reco, selection, CVN, geometry, PID) so it isn't one flavor.
Expected file paths and symbols below are copied verbatim from chunks.jsonl so they match the index exactly.

queries.query: How do I get the CVN classification results for an event?
Where are the hits associated with a cluster retrieved?
How does the CC neutrino event selection pick the highest-energy shower?
How are the track-PID inputs normalized for the CTP (convolutional track PID)?
How does the RegCNN evaluator process / produce results for an event?
How do you check whether two APA wire channels intersect?
Where is reconstructed neutrino energy checked / validated?
